legacy.full_text:
Ballgame. Loser

4-game sweep by the Cubs, 0-7 vs. them this season. 6 losses in a row, 8 L's in their last 9. 34-47 overall. Poor defense and bad errors are the story yet again. On pace for a 68-94 record.

Not going to get any better until someone gets fired, long overdue.

legacy.full_text:
Cubs POBO Jed Hoyer speaks on his trading philosophy with the New York Mets after acquiring David Peterson, who has a 6.09 ERA this season:

“Whenever we see a player on the Mets, we just assume they’d be good literally anywhere else.

He fit that mold.”

(via @JeffPassan) https://t.co/9HlK7ilJj3

legacy.full_text: The MLB trade deadline is not until August 3rd at 6pm. Currently the Mets are not full sellers even if there is a good chance they will be at some point. David Peterson was not contributing. If the Mets are going to turn it around they could definitely do so without Peterson.

result.text:
The MLB trade deadline is not until August 3rd at 6pm. Currently the Mets are not full sellers even if there is a good chance they will be at some point. David Peterson was not contributing. If the Mets are going to turn it around they could definitely do so without Peterson. They didn't lose anybody significant. 

Right now it's hard to say what's going to happen at the deadline for certain but what's becoming increasingly more likely is that they'll become sellers. Especially if things continue as they have this season. 

If they choose to become sellers it's most likely out of anybody on the roster that AJ Minter and Brooks Raley are dealt. They both hit free agency this off season. 

Huascar Brazoban is a little less likely to be traded considering he has control but it's possible. 

Freddy Peralta and Clay Holmes could very easily be traded away as well. It's less likely that this happens but certainly a possibility. Mets are only going to trade Peralta if the year is 100% over to the absolute point of no return (if we aren't at that already) and Clay Holmes needs to be heathy in order for him to be traded. Both hit free agency this off season it could make sense.

It's very likely that Tyrone Taylor and Luis Robert Jr could both be traded at the deadline. Even if the Mets are somewhat competitive these guys are similar to David Peterson. If the Mets are going to turn it around they could do so without them. Both will be free agents this off season and could make sense to be traded. I could also say that same for Jared Young and MJ Melendez even though they have some control left. 

A lot depends on if Bo Bichette will get traded or not. If the Mets are sellers and Bo is playing at a level where it looks like he's going to opt out of his deal in the off season then they'll trade him and get a significant return. Otherwise I expect Bichette to stay with the Mets this year and probably next year too. 

Maybe Sean Manaea or Kodai Senga get traded but I don't see it. Same thing with Luke Weaver even though he'll attract more interest than those two. Manaea could be interesting if the Mets eat money on his deal.  

#LGM
